lede: A look at February 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Leeseo and Ashley Greene, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Prelude to the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine
events.desc: In the prelude to the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine Russian President Vladimir Putin declares the Luhansk People's Republic and Donetsk People's Republic as independent from Ukraine, and moves troops into the region. The action is condemned by the United Nations.

events.title: Suez Canal
events.desc: The last Israeli soldiers leave the west bank of the Suez Canal pursuant to a truce with Egypt.

events.title: Peace symbols
events.desc: The CND symbol, aka peace symbol, commissioned by the Direct Action Committee in protest against the Atomic Weapons Research Establishment, is designed and completed by Gerald Holtom.

events.title: Bengali language movement
events.desc: The Bengali language movement protests occur at the University of Dhaka in East Pakistan (now Bangladesh).

events.title: League of Nations
events.desc: The League of Nations bans foreign national "volunteers" in the Spanish Civil War.

events.title: Kurt Eisner
events.desc: German socialist Kurt Eisner is assassinated. His death results in the establishment of the Bavarian Soviet Republic and parliament and government fleeing Munich, Germany.

events.title: Telephone directory
events.desc: The first telephone directory is issued in New Haven, Connecticut.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Valverde is fought near Fort Craig in New Mexico Territory.

events.title: Cherokee Phoenix
events.desc: Initial issue of the Cherokee Phoenix is the first periodical to use the Cherokee syllabary invented by Sequoyah.

events.title: Ahvenkoski
events.desc: Without a previous declaration of war, Russian troops cross the border to Sweden at Abborfors in eastern Finland, thus beginning the Finnish War, in which Sweden will lose the eastern half of the country (i.e. Finland) to Russia.

births.name: Sahle-Work Zewde
births.line: Ethiopian politician and diplomat, 5th President of Ethiopia

births.name: John Haden Badley
births.line: English author and educator, founded the Bedales School (died 1967)

deaths.text: Cornelius Schnauber, German–American historian, playwright, and academic
Georg Friedrich von Martens, German jurist and diplomat
